Job Summary:
The Corporate Functions Lead Accountant will report and drive results in the accounting functions that support Corporate Finance and the Corporate accounting ledgers. This role will partner with the CoE Finance team to ensure accounting and reporting integrity and effective controls and compliance, confirming consistency to US GAAP and Company policy. He/she will support the reporting and monitoring of actual results and control of financial results consistent with long-term business objectives. This person will be working with peers on the Corporate Functions Controllership team and with the Record to Report (RTR) team assigned to the Corporate ledgers. He/she will provide direction, training and counseling to its various stakeholders on a wide variety of business/financial/accounting issues to ensure achievement and alignment of business processes across the enterprise.

What will you do?
• Proactively works with the Controllership, Corporate FP&A and RTR teams to oversee and review the monthly, quarterly and annual financial results for Corporate ledgers. This includes review of the key reserves and account reconciliations and overall balance sheet and financial metric trends to ensure accuracy and potential gaps.
• Support key projects in the Corporate ledgers to ensure strong financial controls are implemented and maintained.
• Identify and help support movement of activities to the company’s Center of Excellence (e.g. RTR).
• Liaison with cross-functional teams (consolidations, technical accounting, governance, RTR, FP&A, business finance, etc.) and peers to ensure a harmonized environment throughout the Controllership organization.
• Monitor and support the internal control function to ensure controls are adequate, efficient and in line with the Company’s and locally managed policies.
• Support timely execution of Controllership SOX controls along with retention of supporting documentation.
• Participate on cross-functional teams to support financial accounting, reporting and control activities for complex transactions within Corporate (restructurings, supplier contracts, leases, etc.)
• Collaborate with the RTR teams that support the underlying or transactional details.
• Engage and collaborate with the Corporate FP&A team surrounding plan and forecast.
• Proactively identify business/financial opportunities and corrective actions. Support the implementation of these opportunities.
• Provide support for development and enhancement of IT financial and business systems.
• Provide support for all accounting performed at the global business centers for the centralized functions.
• Collaborate with Controllership, FP&A and RTR teams in support of the above objectives.

What we look for?
• Bachelor’s degree in Finance or Accounting, preferably Accounting
• 8+ years progressive finance and accounting experience
• Demonstrated ability to add value to business performance
• Advanced computer skills including SAP, OneStream, Microsoft Office products and financial reporting software
• Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written, and the ability to work effectively with all levels of the organization

Desired Qualifications:
• Public accounting and industry experience
• CPA
• Experience working with remote team members

What we offer:

We offer an exciting and challenging position. Joining us you will become part of a leading global multi-industrial corporation defined by its stimulating work environment and job satisfaction. In addition, we offer outstanding career development opportunities which will stretch your abilities and channel your talents

Diversity & Inclusion Our dedication to diversity and inclusion starts with our values. We lead with integrity and purpose, focusing on the future and aligning with our customers’ vision for success. Our High-Performance Culture ensures that we have the best talent that is highly engaged and eager to innovate.  Our D&I mission elevates each employee’s responsibility to contribute to our culture. It’s through these contributions that we’ll drive the mindsets and behaviors we need to power our customers’ missions. You have the power. You have the voice. You have the culture in your hands.

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. Tasks

Objective:

To manage Monthly, half yearly and annual Closing of Books of accounts, Reporting as per Ind AS and IFRS, co-ordinate with Statutory, Regulators and Internal Audits, submission of various regulatory returns applicable, Monitoring Internal Control systems, ICOFR and Reconciliations.

A. Preparation of financial statements

1. To prepare & Review the Balance sheet, Profit & Loss statements, and schedules under IFRS for month end closure.

2. Review the inputs into the monthly reporting tool and ensure smooth closure of monthly reporting.

3. To prepare financial statements as per IND AS for regulatory reporting.

4. To maintain chart of accounts as per the headquarters guidelines

B. Regulatory Compliance:

1. To prepare various regulatory statutory returns on a weekly, monthly, quarterly, half yearly and on annual basis and submit the same with the regulator on a timely manner.

2. To ensure that the other regulatory certification and declaration such as FDI Certificate, Statutory Auditor’s Certificate are filed with the regulator’s on a timely basis.

3. To support in preparation of data for RBI related queries and Information sought from accounting.

4. To support the Regulator’s inspection from accounting and reporting standpoint.

5. Preparation of Liquidity Coverage Ratios & CRAR on monthly basis.

6. To prepare input for disclosures of various statutory financial and related information on the website of the Company on a timely manner.

7. To prepare and facilitate various regulatory reports for ALCO meetings.

8. To ensure all the relevant and applicable compliances are complied with and closed on a timely manner in the Compliance tool.

C. Audit:

1. To prepare the required balance sheet & P&L schedules as required during the Statutory audit.

2. To work closely with auditors and ensure that the financials contain all relevant disclosures applicable to the Company.

3. To review the audit observations and Management Letter and take it to the relevant team for response and closure.

4. To monitor and ensure the Internal Control Systems are in place as per the Mercedes- Benz Guidelines.

5. To ensure that the Internal Control on Financial Reporting are in place by demonstrating and documenting the controls in place.

6. To prepare presentations for various meetings related to audits like audit committee and board.

D. Accounting Reconciliations:

1. To review the statutory remittances carried out by Taxation and HR department and provide accounting clearance and facilitate nil / insignificant open items.

2. To review the inter-co accounting and confirmation process of all the inter-companies and ensure timely reporting with nil or identified reconciliation items.

3. To review and approve the daily clearing entries.

4. To ensure closure of BRS open items related to accounting.

11. To prepare the dealer balances confirmation and ensure that the same is received on time in coordination with wholesale team.

data.job_description:
At EY, we’re all in to shape your future with confidence.

We’ll help you succeed in a globally connected powerhouse of diverse teams and take your career wherever you want it to go.

Join EY and help to build a better working world.

Senior – FDI

EY – GDS – Consulting – Finance

As part of our EY Finance Consulting Team, you will play a pivotal role in helping clients leverage finance data intelligence and data lake solutions to drive performance improvement initiatives. This techno-functional role involves assessing finance data processes, analysing data pain points, and designing innovative data-driven solutions. You will work with clients across industries and geographies to unlock insights from finance data and enable smarter decision-making.

The Opportunity

We are seeking a Consultant / Senior Consultant with expertise in finance data intelligence, finance data lakes, and finance process – PTP, OTC, RTR & FP&A. Candidates with working knowledge of corporate finance processes, ERP systems (SAP, Oracle, etc.), and finance data analytics are preferred. Experience in FP&A, Financial Accounting, and Reporting domains is an advantage. This is a unique opportunity to contribute to the growth of EY’s finance data intelligence service offering on a global scale.

Your Key Responsibilities
• Expertise on Finance functions including Record to Report (R2R), Procure to Pay (P2P), and Order to Cash (O2C) to understand data flows, account reconciliations, journals, transaction matching, variance analysis, intercompany transactions, and fixed assets accounting.
• Conduct workshops and interviews to perform current state (As-Is) finance data process assessments and design optimized future state processes leveraging data lake architectures and finance data intelligence tools.
• Identify opportunities for process improvement through automation, data integration, technology upgrades, and finance data transformation initiatives.
• Support the implementation and development of finance data lakes, data pipelines, and data governance frameworks.
• Design and implement KPIs, dashboards, and control frameworks for finance data monitoring across O2C, FP&A P2P, and R2R processes.
• Collaborate with stakeholders to gather and translate business requirements into technical data models and solutions.
• Execute data profiling, data quality assessments, and data validation using SQL, Excel, and other analytics tools.
• Maintain comprehensive documentation of finance data processes, data models, and project deliverables.
• Partner with project managers and cross-functional teams to ensure successful delivery of finance data intelligence projects.
• Apply strong accounting knowledge to interpret financial reports and statements in the context of data analytics.
• Understand financial consolidation, cost allocation, finance planning, and budgeting processes as they relate to data integration.
• Support month-end, quarter-end, and year-end financial close activities with data accuracy and reconciliation.
• Possess technical knowledge of ERP systems (SAP ERP/HANA, Oracle GL) and finance data structures.
• Experience in Chart of Accounts (CoA) design and finance data architecture.
• Lead and mentor junior team members on finance data intelligence projects.
• Manage priorities effectively in a dynamic, high-pressure environment.
• Willingness to travel domestically and internationally as required.

Skills And Attributes For Success
• Experience in consulting or finance data intelligence roles is preferred.
• Strong communication skills with the ability to engage confidently with diverse audiences.
• Proficiency in English
• Adaptable and able to work in ambiguous and evolving environments.
• Collaborative team player with the ability to influence and drive consensus.
• Strong analytical mindset with attention to detail and problem-solving skills.
• Knowledge on AI models and understanding of context of GENAI. ML and Agentic in Finance

To Qualify for the Role, You Must Have
• CA, CFA, CPA, MBA, or Master’s degree in Finance or related field.
• 1-2 years (Staff level) or 3-6 years (Senior level) of relevant experience in finance data intelligence, finance analytics, or finance domain.
• Strong skills in Excel, PowerPoint, and SQL .

Ideally, You’ll Also Have
• Exposure to ERP systems (SAP, Oracle), financial consolidation tools (HFM), and finance data lake platforms.
• Experience with finance transformation, ERP implementation, and data governance.
• Project management and stakeholder management capabilities.

What We Look For
• A team player with commercial acumen, technical expertise, and eagerness to learn in a fast-paced environment.
• Opportunities to collaborate with EY Advisory teams globally and work with leading businesses across industries.

What Working At EY Offers

At EY, you will work on inspiring and meaningful projects with some of the world’s top companies. We emphasize education, coaching, and practical experience to support your personal and professional growth. You will have the freedom to shape your career with an individual progression plan and take on challenging assignments in an interdisciplinary environment focused on quality and knowledge sharing. Additionally, we offer:
• Support, coaching, and feedback from engaging colleagues.
• Opportunities to develop new skills and advance your career.
• Flexibility to manage your role in a way that suits you.

EY | Building a better working world

EY is building a better working world by creating new value for clients, people, society and the planet, while building trust in capital markets.

Enabled by data, AI and advanced technology, EY teams help clients shape the future with confidence and develop answers for the most pressing issues of today and tomorrow.

EY teams work across a full spectrum of services in assurance, consulting, tax, strategy and transactions. Fueled by sector insights, a globally connected, multi-disciplinary network and diverse ecosystem partners, EY teams can provide services in more than 150 countries and territories.

data.job_title: Gen AI Credit Risk Analyst and Modeler
data.job_description:
Tiger Analytics is a global leader in AI and analytics, helping Fortune 1000 companies solve their toughest challenges. We offer full-stack AI and analytics services & solutions to empower businesses to achieve real outcomes and value at scale. We are on a mission to push the boundaries of what AI and analytics can do to help enterprises navigate uncertainty and move forward decisively. Our purpose is to provide certainty to shape a better tomorrow.

Our team of 4000+ technologists and consultants are based in the US, Canada, the UK, India, Singapore, and Australia, working closely with clients across CPG, Retail, Insurance, BFS, Manufacturing, Life Sciences, and Healthcare. Many of our team leaders rank in the Top 10 and 40 Under 40 lists, exemplifying our dedication to innovation and excellence.

We are a Great Place to Work-Certified™ ), recognized by analyst firms such as Forrester, Gartner, HFS, Everest, ISG and others. We have been ranked among the ‘Best’ and ‘Fastest Growing’ analytics firms lists by Inc., Financial Times, Economic Times and Analytics India Magazine.

About the role

Tiger Analytics is seeking a Gen AI Credit Risk Analyst and Modeler to join our Banking and Financial Services practice within the Credit Risk team. This role is suited for professionals with strong credit risk domain expertise and hands-on analytical skills, who can also leverage Generative AI and Large Language Models (LLMs) to enhance credit risk analysis, automation, and decision-support workflows.

The role requires close collaboration with business, risk, and compliance stakeholders, translating complex credit risk requirements into scalable analytical and GenAI-enabled solutions. The individual is expected to demonstrate strong technical depth, business acumen, and leadership qualities.

Responsibilities:

Design, develop, and maintain credit risk models across the credit lifecycle including acquisition, underwriting, portfolio monitoring, and loss forecasting. Apply statistical and analytical techniques to solve complex credit risk problems across banking portfolios. Leverage GenAI and LLMs , including prompt engineering, to support risk analysis, automation, documentation, and insight generation. Work closely with business and risk stakeholders to understand requirements and translate them into implementable analytical and GenAI-based solutions. Develop, validate, and document credit risk models in line with regulatory and internal governance standards. Support credit risk strategy initiatives such as scorecard development, policy analytics, segmentation, and portfolio optimization. Communicate analytical insights, model results, and GenAI outputs clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences. Provide technical guidance and mentorship to junior team members.

Required Qualifications, Capabilities and Skills:

Postgraduate degree in Economics, Statistics, Mathematics, Data Science, or a related quantitative discipline. 5+ years of experience in Credit Risk analytics or modeling within the banking or financial services domain. Strong understanding of credit risk concepts including PD, LGD, EAD, underwriting, scorecards, and portfolio risk. Hands-on experience with Python and SQL for data extraction, modeling, and analytics. Practical understanding of Generative AI / LLMs, including prompting techniques and applied business use cases. Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to engage business, risk, and technology partners. Strong problem-solving mindset with the ability to structure ambiguous business problems.

Desired Experience:
Experience in AML, Fraud, or Risk analytics within a banking environment. Exposure to regulatory frameworks such as Basel, IFRS9, CECL , or model risk governance. Experience integrating traditional credit risk models with GenAI-driven workflows. Prior experience in a consulting or client-facing analytics role.

Desirable Skills:
Experience deploying analytics or GenAI solutions into production environments. Ability to translate complex analytical concepts into clear, actionable business recommendations. Strong ownership, leadership mindset, and ability to drive initiatives end-to-end. Curiosity and continuous learning attitude, especially in evolving AI and risk analytics areas.

Job Title: Senior Executive - GST & TDS

Location: Karvenagar, Pune

Reporting to: Chartered Accountant

Department: Taxation

Job Objective:

To manage all compliance, accounting, and operational aspects of GST and TDS independently, ensuring timely

filings, reconciliations, and adherence to applicable laws, rules, and regulations under the Income Tax Act, 1961 and

the CGST Act, 2017 and State GST Acts.

Key Responsibilities:

GST Compliance:

1. End-to-end preparation and filing of:

o GSTR-1, GSTR-3B, GSTR-9 (Annual Return), GSTR-9C (Reconciliation Statement, if applicable).

2. Monthly reconciliation of:

o GSTR-2B with purchase register and books.

o Outward supply vs GSTR-1 vs GSTR-3B vs books.

3. Application of rules w.r.t. RCM, Input Tax Credit (Eligible & Ineligible) while calculating the liability.

4. Calculation of monthly liability and communicating it to the clients for making payments.

5. Preparation of challans and Ensuring Timely payment of GST liability.

6. Maintain records for GST audits and departmental inspections.

7. Handling submissions of notices, scrutiny, audit proceedings, and liaisoning with the GST department.

8. Maintenance of Input Tax Credit registers and reconciliation with books of accounts.

TDS Compliance:

1. Timely deduction and deposit of TDS/TCS under the Income Tax Act.

2. Preparation and filing of quarterly TDS returns – 24Q, 26Q, 27Q, etc. and corrections thereof, if any.

3. Preparation of TDS challans and reconciliation of TDS ledgers.

4. Timely issuance of TDS certificates in Form 16 and 16A.

5. Preparation of Form 15CA/15CB data for foreign remittances in coordination with CA (where required).

6. Managing lower/NIL deduction certificates and applicability thereof.

7. Handling TDS-related notices from CPC-TDS/Income Tax Department.

8. Tracking PAN compliance and validation for vendors and employees.

✓ Working Practical knowledge of GST laws, rules, procedures, and portal functionalities.

✓ Proficiency in accounting software (Tally, SAP, etc.), MS Excel, and GST/TDS utilities.

✓ Ability to interpret tax laws and apply them to business transactions.

✓ Strong analytical, reconciliation, and communication skills.

✓ Self-motivated and able to handle responsibilities independently.
